data.description:
The Problem
  
  
36 million businesses in America need insurance—it’s not optional.
**77% are underinsured.**
40% have no coverage at all. The distribution system failed them: too slow, too opaque, too confusing.
  
  
Over 90% of commercial insurance is still human-led. We’re building the inverse: 90%+ AI-led, pushing toward the higher 90s. Not by patching legacy workflows—by building AI that makes humans more effective, improves the customer experience, and eliminates friction at every step.
  
  
We’re adding ~1,000 customers per month. We’ve grown 100x since last year. We’re looking to do even more this year—and that’s why we’re hiring.
  
  
**To grow that fast, we need to understand—with precision—what’s working, what’s not, and why.**
The Thesis
  
  
The line between “analytics” and “product” barely exists here. Your models will directly power how our agents prioritize leads, time outreach, and personalize conversations. You’ll build the intelligence that tells us how well it’s working—and ship code that directly moves revenue.
  
  
The Role
  
  
You’ll own the metrics, analytics, and experimentation infrastructure that powers growth. This isn’t “build dashboards and wait for questions.” You’ll define the KPIs that matter, instrument systems to track them, and ship code that directly moves revenue.
  
  
You work across paid acquisition channels—Google Ads, Meta, TikTok—combining it with product analytics and using AI to surface insights that would take others weeks to find.
  
  
What You’ll Do
  
  

- Build metrics and KPI infrastructure — Define, instrument, and own the metrics that matter in real time
- Own LTV/CAC systems — Track unit economics across verticals, channels, and cohorts
- Build paid channel analytics — Connect ad spend to actual revenue, not vanity metrics
- Create attribution that works — Multi-touch attribution across voice, email, web, and referral
- Use AI for insight generation — Pattern detection, anomaly detection, automated analysis
- Ship experimentation infrastructure — A/B testing with statistical rigor

You Might Be a Fit If…
  
  

- You ship code—production code, not just notebooks (Python, SQL)
- You’ve defined KPIs, built instrumentation, and been accountable for moving them
- You’ve worked with paid acquisition data (Google Ads, Meta, TikTok)
- You use AI to accelerate analysis (PostHog or similar)
- You’ve built GTM systems: lead scoring, attribution, LTV/CAC analysis
- You think in experiments and know correlation vs. causation
- You’re 2-5 years into your career

**Requirements**

- 2-5 years experience in analytics engineering or growth engineering
- Strong Python and SQL skills
- Experience with paid acquisition data and funnel analytics
- Ability to ship production code, not just analysis
- Experience with A/B testing and experimentation
- Based in San Francisco or willing to relocate

Nice to Have
  
  

- Experience with PostHog, Amplitude, or similar product analytics
- Background in lead scoring or attribution modeling
- Prior startup or high-growth company experience

**Compensation**

- Salary: $130,000–$190,000 + performance bonuses & equity
- Location: San Francisco, in-office

**Benefits**

- Health, dental, and vision insurance
- Commuter benefits
- Team meals and snacks

The Process
  
  

- 15-min founder call — Alignment on mission and pace
- Technical conversation — Walk us through analysis you’ve done
- On-site — Meet the team, see the data

To Apply
  
  
Data talks. Narratives walk. If you prove things instead of just believing them—send your resume and an example of analysis that drove a business decision.
  
  
Compensation Range: $130K - $190K

About Zerve

Zerve is an agentic data workspace for decision-grade data work.

We’re building infrastructure that allows teams to move from exploratory analysis to repeatable decision systems.

Our early users include quantitative trading firms and advanced enterprise data teams building agentic workflows on top of their data.

The Role

We’re hiring a Founding Growth Engineer to build Zerve’s growth engine from the ground up.

This is not a traditional marketing role.

You’ll sit at the intersection of product, engineering, and distribution, helping turn real workflows built on Zerve into demos, tutorials, templates, and narratives that drive adoption.

You’ll work directly with the founders to define how Zerve shows up in the world as a new category of product.

What You’ll Do

• Build Zerve’s growth engine through technical content, demos, and workflow examples

• Turn real customer workflows into tutorials, templates, and reusable artifacts

• Create technical articles and product explainers for the data and AI community

• Experiment with growth loops embedded directly into the product

• Develop distribution across developer and data science communities

• Work closely with early users to produce case studies and workflow guides

What We’re Looking For

You’re a technical storyteller and builder who enjoys working close to the product.

You likely have experience in one or more of the following:

• Technical writing or developer content

• Growth engineering or product-led growth

• Data science, developer tools, or AI platforms

• Startup environments where you built programs from scratch

You enjoy explaining complex systems clearly and helping products reach the right audience.

Nice to Have

• Experience with data science tools or AI platforms

• Familiarity with developer communities

• Experience running content programs or technical newsletters

• Interest in financial markets or quantitative systems

Why Join Zerve

You’ll help shape how a new category of AI systems emerges.

Instead of generating one-off outputs, the next generation of tools will power systems that continuously reason over data and drive decisions.

Zerve is building the workspace where those systems are created.

I’m Cole, Head of Demand Generation at Plain. We’re redefining customer support for the next generation of B2B companies. We’re building the fastest, most powerful platform to help companies move beyond reactive support and build true customer relationships.
  
  
Some of the world’s most forward-thinking companies, like Cursor, Ashby, and Sanity, trust Plain to unify all customer interactions, enable faster team collaboration, and supercharge their workflows with AI.
  
  
We’re a small, tight-knit crew with offices in SF and London. This role is hybrid with 3 days a week in our SF office.
  
  
Why this role is special
  
  
B2B customer support is undergoing a seismic shift. AI is transforming the way companies engage with customers, shifting support from a siloed function to a company-wide effort across Slack, Discord, and in-product experiences.
  
  
Plain is leading the charge in building this new world, and the GTM Engineer owns how we turn that vision into pipeline across paid, outbound, and content. You'll have real budget, real autonomy, and a major finger on the scale of revenue.
  
  
You'll be one of the first hires on a GTM team we're building from scratch. The vision is a small, technical, high-output team that treats marketing like a product: instrumented, iterative, and AI-native.
  
  
What you’ll do
  
  
You’ll spend 100% of your time generating pipeline with minimal guardrails and a lot of room to experiment.
  
  
Day to day, you will:
  
  

- Build and scale repeatable signal-based outbound to generate net-new pipeline through experimentation and optimizations.
- Create LLM-powered content that ranks highly in SEO and AI search (we call this Generative Engine Optimization aka GEO).
- Report on successes and improvements with constant iteration.
- Write copy that converts — landing pages, ads, and emails that feel like they were written by someone who actually understands the product and buyer.
- Instrument everything so we know what’s working (and what’s not), and use that feedback loop to ship better, faster.

This is a great fit if you…
  
  

- You have strong sales acumen and write copy that converts (without AI) - landing pages, ads, and emails that feel like they were written by someone who understands the product and buyer.
- You’ve been testing and honing your skills with the latest AI and workflow automation tools (n8n, Unify, Claude Code, Clay, Instantly, etc.)
- Have a builder mentality and care more about what we can ship this week than a perfect Q4 plan.
- Are a little technical and very curious — comfortable with APIs, light coding, and connecting systems to unlock new GTM motions.
- Are an obsessive experimenter who is happiest when testing, iterating, and letting results guide your time and energy.

This won’t be the right role for you if you…
  
  

- Need a detailed playbook handed to you instead of helping write it. We’ve had success, but are very much an early stage company looking for creativity and differentiation.
- Optimize for process over output.
- Prefer "big company" environments over fast-moving, hands-on work.

The salary range for the role is $150k - $180k + equity. The salary range shown is intentionally broad to reflect the range of skills and levels that we are open to for this role. Your placement within in the salary range will be decided upon completion of the interview process, taking into account factors like your demonstrated skills, and the depth of your experience. Our Recruiting team will be able to provide more details during the interview process.
  
  
Compensation Range: $150K - $180K

# **GTM Lead Engineer**

**Reports to:** Chief Customer Officer  
**Location:** London (preference) or New York. Both 4 days a week onsite.

## **The Mission**

We have a product that works, a team delivering results, and a PLG motion converting prosumer users into enterprise contracts — all without a systematic GTM function. Now it's time to build one.

Your mission: design, build, and run the revenue engine that takes us to $100M ARR. You are equal parts strategist, systems builder, and operator — the CTO of our go-to-market motion.

## **The Role**

We've grown fast on inbound and product. What comes next is the precision layer on top — the systems, signals, and automation that turn a fast-growing company into a high-frequency revenue machine.

You'll design our outbound infrastructure, engineer AI-powered prospecting workflows, and build the signal layer that tells Sales and CS exactly where to focus. In the early days, you'll run the motion yourself. As we scale, you'll own the architecture that powers everyone else.

## **What You'll Own**

**Outbound Infrastructure** — Build the full outbound stack from scratch: email domains, deliverability, sending tools, CRM workflows, enrichment pipelines. Full autonomy on tooling decisions.

**AI-Powered Prospecting** — Clay-powered enrichment waterfalls, intent signals, lead scoring, and personalised outreach at scale. Define the ICP, source and segment target account lists.

**GTM Automation** — Eliminate manual work across the full sales and CS motion. Automate prep, follow-up, and note-taking so the team spends time on what actually moves revenue.

**Signal Layer** — Surface churn risk, expansion triggers, and pipeline health automatically. Build briefings and alerts so Sales and CS always know where to focus — without asking for a report.

**Stack Architecture** — Own the strategic view of the GTM tech stack. Cut what's redundant, add what's missing, turn a list of tools into a coherent system.

## **What Success Looks Like at Six Months**

- A live outbound engine generating measurable pipeline
- Automated churn, expansion, and pipeline signals delivered to Sales and CS
- At least one AI-powered workflow demonstrably moving a revenue metric
- The most painful manual work designed out, not delegated
- You're setting the agenda, not waiting for one

## **Who You Are**

- Technical GTM operator with deep knowledge of the modern stack — Clay, Instantly/Smartlead, Apollo, Gong, CRM automation
- Builder, not just configurer — you've done this the hard way and now use AI to do it ten times faster
- Commercial and technical in equal measure — you connect data signals to revenue outcomes
- Systems thinker — when you see a manual process, your instinct is to automate it
- Able to code or script automations and work with APIs without needing engineering support
- 5–10 years in GTM engineering, RevOps, or outbound-focused growth at a B2B SaaS company

## **Why Fyxer?**

We believe a small, exceptional team working with extreme focus will always outperform a large, bloated organisation.

- **Autonomy** — real ownership from day one
- **Velocity** — senior responsibility far earlier than anywhere else
- **Scale** — on track to hit $100M ARR by end of 2026; join at the foundational stage

Compensation Range: £120K - £160K

- 10x DAU growth within the next 12 months by doing GEO/SEO-focused software engineering development.
- Own frontend development for BeyondStyle across desktop and mobile platforms.
- Drive organic traffic growth through SEO and GEO-focused frontend engineering

Pay: $20.00 per hour

Expected hours: 10 - 20 per week

Schedule: Monday to Friday

Work Location: Onsite (preferred) or Hybrid remote in Sunnyvale, CA 94086

What You Should Already Have

- Strong proficiency in React, Vue, or similar modern frameworks.
- 3+ years of professional frontend development experience.
- Hands-on experience with GEO and SEO.

What You Will Get

- Become a part of a global, diverse team and grow your expertise and career in a sustainable, high-growth project.

Full-time preferred

Senior Individual Contributor (contractor flexible)

**Reports to:**
Director of Engineering

April 2026

**About Sunny**

Sunny is a purpose-driven social well-being startup helping people unlock more fulfilling social

relationships. As an innovative not-for-profit that operates like an early-stage technology

company, our mission is to create a more meaningfully connected world through a science-led

approach. Social well-being is clinically proven to be a key driver of mental and physical health.

We are privately funded and well-resourced, positioned to drive significant, positive change with

a long-term commitment to achieve sustained impact.

**Role summary**

This role exists to make Sunny a learning machine. You will service both the
**Product team and**

**Research & Development team**
as an Individual Contributor, helping us validate what works

fast while keeping the platform reliable.

This is a senior, hands-on Individual Contributor role for someone who is entrepreneurial,

autonomous, and obsessed with solving problems end-to-end. You will build across web, integrations, data pipelines, experimentation, and embedding AI into existing user workflows. You will also support Engineering customer-facing interactions when needed (onboarding, bugs, etc.).

**What you’ll own**

You will own the build-measure-learn engine: product analytics, rapid prototyping,

experimentation, activation improvements, and fast iteration in Slack, Microsoft Teams, and Web apps. You will also strengthen the technical foundation required for business-to-business deployments.

**Core responsibilities**

- Improve the activation funnel end-to-end
- Implement analytics tracking across the web app, Slack, Microsoft Teams experiences, and the overall AI experience
- Define the event taxonomy (what actions are tracked), ensure data quality, and create lightweight dashboards used weekly for decision-making
- Ship measurable experiments weekly using feature flags, A/B tests, staged rollouts, and holdouts
- Build rapid prototypes and internal automations by leveraging agentic coding and no-code tools
- Build and improve third-party integrations and onboarding workflows (human resources information systems, applicant tracking systems, identity providers)
- Support customer onboarding when needed: data import, single sign-on configuration, integration setup, and troubleshooting
- Partner closely with Product, Design, Growth, and Client Experience to translate pilot learnings into shipped improvements

**Required tools and working style**

This role requires comfort using modern agentic coding and no-code tooling to move quickly and automate repetitive work.

**Required**

- Use of agentic coding and no-code tools for rapid prototyping and automation, such as Cursor, Claude Code, Github Co-pilot, Linear, OpenClaw, Builder.io, Lovable, n8n, or equivalent tooling.

- Senior full-stack engineer with 4+ years building and shipping production products
- Entrepreneurial experience required: self-starter with strong agency, autonomy, and end-to-end problem ownership
- Strong product analytics fluency: event design, funnels, cohorts, retention curves, and experiment readouts
- Hands-on experience implementing experimentation systems (feature flags, A/B testing, holdouts, staged rollouts)
- Experience building onboarding and activation flows for business software (business-to-business software)
- Strong written communication and ability to work independently in a fast-moving environment

**Technical experience**

- Event-driven architectures and real-time workflows
- Third-party integrations and schema mapping, ideally with APIs such as Workday, BambooHR, ADP, Greenhouse (or similar)

- Multi-tenant stability improvements and tenant isolation

**About Drafted**

Drafted is unlocking creativity in the physical world. We’re building foundational models and generative pipelines that create floor plans and renderings instantly, so anyone can imagine their dream home. Starting with single-family homes, we plan to verticalize across all dimensions of the pre-construction stack.

Generative architecture is a prime domain for applied research with abundant data, verifiable constraints, and a clear value proposition. Our team of second-time founders, engineers, and designers pair exceptional product taste with deep technical rigor to turn real-world buildability constraints into an intuitive, creative experience.

**Drafted's Values**

We're a small team working fully in-person in San Francisco. We value high-ownership builders who want to be a part of a talented, highly motivated team. We're guided by the following values:

- **Own the mission.**
  We take agency, act like owners, and see problems through to real outcomes.
- **Build in the open.**
  We value direct feedback, fast learning, and growth through honest collaboration.
- **Move with care and speed.**
  We iterate quickly while staying deeply respectful of our teammates.
- **Seek the why.**
  We challenge assumptions, think from first principles, and never stop asking questions.
- **Design for everyone.**
  We believe anyone should be able to design and build a home they love.
- **Solve what matters.**
  We embrace hard problems and create new paths forward when none exist.

**The Role**

As a growth engineer, you'll play an important role in strengthening our product led growth strategy. You'll work closely with the product and engineering teams to identify growth opportunities from the product usage and embed growth loops into the product's development. The ideal person for this role dives deep into prospective growth opportunities, and is comfortable starting them manually to validate and scale them into automated growth loops.

**Example Projects**

- Build automated flywheels that turn content generated on the site to social media ready content for Pinterest and Instagram

- Trial viral content formats and embed the best formulas to be created and shareable via export within the product

**Desired Skillset**

- 5+ years coding and/or growth / performance marketing experience

- Experience building repeatable automations and workflows to scale growth loops

Salary Range

**About Sim**
Sim is building the leading platform for AI agents—enabling developers and enterprises to visually create, launch, and manage high-performing agents that automate the world’s workflows. Our mission is simple: agents will run the world, and Sim is the primary means by which that happens. We’re backed by world-class investors including Standard Capital, Paul Graham, Perplexity, SV Angel, and Y Combinator, and we’ve been open source from day one.
  
  
We're a small, fast-moving team based in San Francisco, building together in person every day. 70,000+ developers use Sim; your work ships fast and impacts real users at scale. We believe in ownership, excellence, and ambitious builders shaping the future from the ground up.
  
  
**About The Role**
This is a foundational role: Sim is hiring our first GTM Engineer to build the company’s go-to-market and RevOps infrastructure from the ground up. You won’t just optimize existing workflows—you'll architect them. You’ll partner directly with leadership to define, implement, and scale our GTM systems, automations, and data flows. If you want to operate as a technical founder for our GTM function and ship quickly with massive ownership, this role is for you.
  
  
You'll work full-time, in-person, out of our San Francisco office.
  
  
**What You'll Do**

- Build from zero: Create Sim’s GTM infrastructure from scratch—integrating best-in-class tools like Salesforce, Clay, Gong, enrichment APIs, and more into a seamless automated engine
- Invent internal automation: Design and build AI agents and custom workflows to automate account research, minimize manual tasks, and personalize outreach at scale
- Engineer data flows: Architect the middleware and webhooks that ensure data integrity and sync between Sim’s product, CRM, and internal systems
- Programmatic GTM: Translate top-level GTM strategy into automated workflows, custom internal tools, and data-driven outbound campaigns
- Found and scale: Set the technical standard and shape the DNA of Sim’s GTM/Revenue Operations function for years to come

**What We’re Looking For**

- 3+ years of experience in GTM Engineering, technical RevOps, or similar technical roles
- Proficiency writing code (Python, JavaScript, or SQL) and familiarity working with APIs
- Track record of integrating, automating, and scaling SaaS and AI tool stacks
- Strong understanding of sales funnels, GTM business logic, and operations
- Experience with modern GTM tools (Clay, Claude Code, Parallel, Unify, Sim, scraping/indexing the web, etc.)
- High bias toward action and "shipping v1 today"—you thrive in ambiguity and fast-paced teams
- Deep ownership mentality; you want end-to-end responsibility for Sim’s GTM architecture and future team

**Why Sim**

- Foundational impact: You’ll build and define Sim’s GTM infrastructure and shape how agents are sold, deployed, and supported
- Immediate scale: Your work ships to 70,000+ developers and customers right now
- World-class backers: Series A led by Standard Capital, with Paul Graham, Perplexity, SV Angel, and Y Combinator
- Open source culture: 26,000+ GitHub stars and a passionate developer community
- Competitive compensation: Competitive pay + meaningful equity
- Full benefits: Comprehensive health insurance and relocation support
- Visa sponsorship available

**Equal Opportunity**
Sim is committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all team members. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

**About Giga**
Giga has recently raised a $61M Series A and has several paying customers, including DoorDash. We’re building the next generation of customer experience - real-time AI agents that can understand emotion, resolve issues instantly, and scale across the world’s largest enterprises.
  
  
It’s an exciting inflection point for the company. While we have been successful, we have larger ambitions. Our goal is to become the go-to AI platform for all enterprise automation, powered by our voice superintelligence. To achieve this, we need more great engineers.
  
  
The work affects millions of people every day and our engineers have autonomy and make true impact. This opportunity is unique because we have brilliant founders, have found commercial success, and see a clear path to becoming a generational company. Some further info about us:
  
  

- Voice AI startup Giga raises $61M Series A
- DoorDash and Giga Partnership

Giga builds AI agents trusted by the largest B2C companies in the world. Industry leaders like DoorDash rely on Giga to automate their most complex support and operations workflows across voice, chat, and email. If being a part of this resonates with you, please apply!
  
  
**About The Role**
Giga is seeking a GTM Engineer to architect, automate, and scale the systems that power Giga's revenue engine. This role is for someone who is growth minded and technical. They understand business context and requirements, formulate unique ideas and perspectives, then go execute them from end to end.
  
  
Working closely with Sales, Sales Development, Solutions Engineering, Marketing, and RevOps leadership, you'll own the technical infrastructure that turns go-to-market strategy into pipeline. You'll integrate modern technology solutions, build with AI, engineer data flows, and deploy intelligent automation across the full GTM motion - from prospect signal detection to customer renewal.
  
  
The person in this role will make sure our own go-to-market runs with the same precision and intelligence we deliver to our customers. This person must be a self-starter and be able to work with high-autonomy - you'll be building the revenue engine for a company whose customers trust us to talk to millions of their customers every day.
  
  
**What You'll Do**

- Architect the GTM tech stack: Build an AI native tech stack with seamless integrations. Build a cohesive, automated engine that centralizes data in CRM and eventually data warehouse. (Salesforce, Gong, Clay, Claude Cowork)
- Automate what slows us down: Identify repetitive work across the GTM org - account research, CRM hygiene, post-call follow-ups - and replace it with intelligent automation using APIs, webhooks, and AI agents.
- Own data integrity end-to-end: Build and maintain the data flows, enrichment logic, and sync architecture that keep our systems accurate, deduped, and trustworthy.
- Turn strategy into systems: Partner with leadership to translate GTM strategy into automated playbooks, dynamic scoring models, and signal-based workflows that drive measurable revenue outcomes.
- Systematic pipeline generation: Build the outbound engine from scratch - enrichment logic, intent triggers, AI-personalized touchpoints, and human review checkpoints - all wired to a single output: qualified meetings booked.

**You May Be a Good Fit If You:**

- Have 3–5+ years of experience operating in a Growth, GTM Engineer, or Revenue Operations role. We are open to candidates that have a technical background that have worked with a GTM team in other capacities.
- Are a builder and systems thinker: You want to build moonshot ideas, not just toggle settings. You're the person who sees a manual process and immediately starts thinking about the API call or AI agent that replaces it. You see the GTM stack as an interconnected machine, not a collection of point solutions.
- Constant iteration: Be a Kaizen methodology champion by building, shipping and iterating fast. You thrive in ambiguity, operate with high ownership, and move fast without waiting for perfect requirements. But you know when to slow down and ensure you’re shipping the highest quality work possible.
- Are technically capable: Comfortable working with APIs, webhooks, and data integrations. You've worked with Python, JavaScript, or SQL in a professional context and can navigate technical documentation without hand-holding.
- Are GTM-fluent: You understand the GTM funnel - not just the data model, but the logic behind every stage. You know what actually converts pipeline because you've worked in or alongside GTM teams.

**Strong Candidates May Also Have:**

- Background at a fast-moving B2B SaaS / AI startup or even in an agency landscape helping similar companies
- Hands-on experience with CRM’s and Clay, bonus points for building data warehouses
- Experience designing enrichment waterfalls and managing complex data sourcing across multiple providers
- Experience centralizing selling signals from multiple data sources into a single location
- You've built or deployed AI agents for GTM use cases - from ideation to realization
- You've automated an outbound or inbound motion end-to-end and can point to the pipeline it created

**Perks & Benefits**

- Competitive equity
- Catered lunch daily + dinner stipend
- $150/month wellness benefit (gym, fitness classes, mental health)
- Medical, dental, and vision coverage
- 401(k) plan
- Commuter benefits

Giga is an equal opportunity employer. We're committed to providing equal employment opportunities regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.
  
  
Compensation Range: $200K - $240K

Orb is transforming how modern AI and software companies monetize at scale. We've built the next-generation billing infrastructure that turns complex usage-based pricing into competitive advantage. Our developer-first approach powers companies like Vercel, Pinecone, and Replit, delivering real-time billing automation, lightning-fast pricing experiments, and granular revenue analytics.
  
  
Backed by $44.1M in funding from top investors like Mayfield, Menlo Ventures, and Greylock, we're a high-velocity team shipping infrastructure that's defining the future of monetization.
  
  
For SF Bay Area and NYC residents, we've built a hybrid work culture where we are in office 3 days/week. For remote roles, we ask you to join us in person 1x per quarter. Our values,
*customer centricity, minutes matter, run with it, and attention to detail*
shape how we work and grow as a team.
  
  
**About The Role**
As an early engineer at Orb, you’ll help to build the technical foundations of a critical product that needs to be reliable and accurate at scale. You’ll work closely with our customers to understand pain points in their business processes, and help incorporate those insights into our product roadmap. Our customers rely on Orb to transform an inherently complex domain (billing, from data ingestion to subscription management and revenue recognition) into an efficient, powerful, and easy-to-use experience.
  
  
**In This Role You Will**

- Design and maintain core pricing lifecycle primitives with a focus on extensibility and ease of use
- Evolve the product data model to balance complexity and clarity
- Scale real-time ingestion and query systems for large, high-performance deployments
- Collaborate cross-functionally (PM, design, sales, customer success) to shape strategy and deliver value
- Learn from and contribute to a highly skilled team

**About You**

- 8+ years of experience in software engineering
- Enthusiastic about working on a broad range of technical challenges across the stack

- Based in the San Francisco Bay Area or willing to relocate (with support from Orb)

**Orb’s Tech Stack**
At Orb, we believe great engineers can learn any stack, so you do not need experience with these specific tools, and we encourage you to apply regardless.

**Job Title**
: Staff Software Engineer, Revenue Platform
  
  
**Role Overview**
Join a rapidly scaling, venture-backed AI-powered commerce platform that is redefining how transactions happen in the physical world. This organization is building next-generation infrastructure that powers seamless, real-world payments and monetization at scale.
  
  
As a Staff Software Engineer on the Revenue Platform team, you will own the technical direction of billing, payments, and invoicing systems that process tens of millions in monthly transactions. This is a high-impact, high-ownership role where your work directly shapes monetization strategy, financial accuracy, and expansion into new verticals.
  
  
You’ll operate at the intersection of engineering, finance, and operations, balancing near-term execution with long-term architectural excellence. If you enjoy designing resilient distributed systems where correctness truly matters, this is a foundational role with real business impact.
  
  
**Key Responsibilities**

- Own the architecture and evolution of billing, payments, and invoicing infrastructure
- Establish standards for financial accuracy, transaction integrity, and compliance
- Design resilient multi-gateway payment processing with intelligent routing, retry logic, and graceful failure handling
- Build monitoring and reporting tools for revenue operations including reconciliation dashboards, payment success metrics, and audit trails
- Partner cross-functionally with finance and operations to automate dispute resolution, refund workflows, and reconciliation processes
- Drive best practices for idempotent payment processing, distributed transaction handling, audit logging, and financial system testing
- Own integrations with payment gateways, ERP systems, accounting platforms, and analytics tools
- Influence system-wide reliability standards and mentor teams on revenue systems engineering patterns

**Education & Qualifications**

- 8+ years of hands-on software engineering experience
- Deep experience building revenue systems such as payment platforms, billing systems, or invoicing infrastructure
- Strong systems thinking with expertise in distributed financial systems and handling edge cases such as duplicate requests, partial failures, currency conversion, tax logic, and timing issues
- Advanced database knowledge including transaction isolation, audit logging, financial data migrations, and high-availability systems requiring absolute correctness
- Production experience integrating with payment gateways such as Stripe, PayPal, or similar processors
- Experience navigating compliance requirements such as PCI-DSS
- Strong cross-functional communication skills, especially with finance, accounting, and data teams

- Strong Java expertise with understanding of JVM performance and concurrency

data.description:
**Company Description**

Casper Studios is an AI services partnering with enterprises, startups, and creators to build thoughtful, AI-driven products. With a team of builders from LinkedIn, Bain, and Microsoft, we help companies unlock new opportunities through AI and sharp product design.

We’ve been able to bootstrap the company to mid– to late–single digit millions in revenue largely through founder-led sales. That approach has worked well, but as the company grows we need stronger systems behind how we operate go-to-market.

Beyond client work, we also build our own products—like our Chrome extension used by over 20,000 people and newer voice-based AI tools. Casper is a profitable, bootstrapped, global team. We reinvest our profits into our people and our products. The goal is simple: work with smart people, solve real problems, and build durable businesses.

**Why This Role Exists**

As Casper grows, our go-to-market processes are becoming more complex. We’ve already built a number of internal automations across our sales and operating workflows, but today they live in different places and are mostly maintained by founders and engineers.

We’re looking for someone who can take ownership of these systems and turn them into a coherent, scalable GTM infrastructure.

A key goal of this role is giving time back to our partners and senior leaders by automating large parts of the go-to-market process. That includes things like proposal generation, pipeline visibility, outreach workflows, and internal reporting.

This person will help centralize, maintain, and expand the automations that power how Casper sources, tracks, and closes new work.

**What You’ll Do**

You will take ownership of Casper’s go-to-market systems and automations.

A core part of the role will involve managing and extending our CRM infrastructure. We currently use Attio and expect this person to become highly proficient in it quickly, even if the system itself evolves over time.

You will also work heavily with tools like Claude Code to automate workflows across our business.

Examples of projects you may work on include:

- Proposal generation systems that automatically assemble client proposals from past work and templates
- Pipeline visibility and review systems so leadership can easily understand deal flow
- Account and network mapping across our relationships and partners
- Automated outreach workflows across platforms such as LinkedIn
- Content creation systems that help the team publish insights and case studies more consistently
- Internal automation that reduces manual work across business development and operations

The goal is to create a system where much of Casper’s GTM motion becomes assisted or automated, allowing the team to focus on relationships.

**Who You Are**

- You enjoy building systems that make organizations run better.
- You likely have experience in areas like revenue operations, growth engineering, GTM engineering, sales operations, or automation engineering.
- You are comfortable working with CRMs and revenue systems. Experience with tools like Attio, HubSpot, or Salesforce is helpful, but what matters most is your ability to design systems that make pipeline and customer relationships easier to manage.
- You are comfortable building automations using APIs, workflow tools, or scripting environments. Experience using developer-oriented tools such as Claude Code to automate workflows is a strong plus.
- You also have a commercial instinct. You understand how sales teams operate and how pipeline, relationships, and timing affect deals.
- You enjoy experimenting with new tools and figuring out how to connect systems in ways that remove friction from how teams operate.

**What Success Looks Like**

- Within the first six months, this person will have taken ownership of Casper’s GTM systems and made them significantly more cohesive.
- You will have built workflows that automate large parts of proposal generation, outreach, pipeline tracking, and reporting.
- You will also have created systems that give leadership clear visibility into pipeline health while reducing the operational overhead required to run the business.

**Compensation & Structure**

This is a full-time role with competitive compensation and bonus structure. Casper operates as a global, remote-friendly team and this role offers the opportunity to build the operational backbone of a fast-growing AI company.

data.description:
**Company Description**

Software Toolbox is a full-stack industrial AI company that specializes in delivering innovative industrial connectivity software, data platforms, and AI agents to support manufacturing and process industries. The company's advanced solutions, such as TOP Server, OPC Router, and OmniServer, are recognized for exceptional customer support and high customer satisfaction. With offerings like N3uron and Cogent DataHub, Software Toolbox enables customers to securely transport and contextualize data for effective industrial operations. The company also delivers cutting-edge AI capabilities like JonJon.ai, a specialized agent for addressing industrial challenges and providing actionable insights. Serving leading global businesses across diverse industries such as automotive, chemicals, and power generation, Software Toolbox empowers clients to navigate their industrial AI transformation.

**Outcome / Mission**

Our customers come first. We’re committed to setting them up for success by meeting them wherever they are in their Industrial Connectivity journey. Our goal is to make every interaction informative, effective, and genuinely enjoyable.

In this role, you will build and operate our outbound and lifecycle email marketing engine, delivering the right message at the right time to the right audience. Your focus will be on guiding prospects through their journey and consistently converting qualified targets into booked demos and self-serve purchases.

**What you’ll do (core responsibilities)**

**•**
Build segmented email programs & campaigns (ICP-based), A/B test messaging, manage deliverability, and report funnel impact.

• Own lead routing, scoring, and handoff (marketing → SDR/AE), plus meeting booking workflows.

• Stand up conversion instrumentation: review copy, create graphics (AI enabled), secure landing pages, forms, UTMs, attribution, dashboards.

• Run weekly experimentation cadence: subject lines, sequences, CTAs, landing pages, nurture leads.

• Lead on consistent adjustments to build and improve connection with our customers.

• Owns email marketing end-to-end, collaborates with Marketing, ECommerce and Sales teams.

**Required (must-have)**

• 3–6+ years in lifecycle/demand gen and/or marketing ops with hands-on campaign execution.

• Strong ops chops: CRM + marketing automation + data hygiene + reporting (Experience with Hubspot)

• Proven ability to move pipeline metrics (meetings set, conversion rate, CAC payback signals).

• Excellent writing with an engineering mindset (hypothesis → test → measure →iterate)

• Strong work ethic

**Preferred**

• Experience marketing to industrial/OT personas (Ops/Engineering/IT) and technical products.

• Familiarity with account-based motions (ABM-lite), personalization at scale, and intent signals.

• Basic technical literacy: APIs/webhooks, enrichment, SQL-lite or data tooling comfort.

Location

• Our HQ is located in Charlotte, NC but we are looking for the best talent across the United States.

• International candidates will be considered based on expertise and experience.

data.description:
**The Company**

Duende Software is defining the identity infrastructure category. Trusted by more than 2,500 organizations worldwide, Duende and our flagship product, IdentityServer, help the world’s most discerning organizations secure the identities of their users.  After a decade of open source innovation, Duende IdentityServer was launched in 2020 to provide the world’s most customizable, extensible identity infrastructure for securing human and machine access and identity across modern .NET ecosystems and beyond. 

With accelerating demand, strong financial performance, and a product roadmap expanding into federation, non-human identity, and modern developer tooling, Duende is now building the operational infrastructure to match its engineering ambition. An outstanding Revenue Operations Engineer is central to that mission.

**The Role**

We are looking for a Revenue Operations Engineer to design, build, and automate the systems that power our go-to-market engine. 

You will own the technical architecture of our HubSpot ecosystem and the operational workflows that connect Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, and Finance. You will build the automation that replaces manual processes, design the data models that make reporting trustworthy, and create the integrations that connect our tools into a cohesive system.

This role owns the integrity, performance, and evolution of our HubSpot ecosystem across the Revenue and Marketing teams. You will design and refine the architecture that supports opportunity progression, lifecycle management, forecasting, attribution, and executive reporting.

Duende’s engineering team is AI-forward and technically sophisticated. Our go-to-market (GTM) operations are not - yet. Your job is to close that gap: bring engineering rigor, automation-first thinking, and AI-assisted tooling to the systems that track pipeline, manage renewals, and drive revenue growth. 

This role will report to the Head of Sales & Renewals, but will partner closely with Marketing, Customer Success, Finance and Operations. 

**What You Will Build**

This is not a maintenance role. It is a build-and-scale role.

**Revenue Systems Architecture**

- Design and implement the HubSpot architecture for a new pricing and packaging rollout: custom objects, deal stages, properties, pipeline views, and automated quote workflows
- Build and maintain executive dashboards and forensic reporting across pipeline velocity, win/loss analysis, cohort performance, and revenue forecasting
- Own CRM data integrity: schema design, deduplication, data mapping, and governance practices that establish a single source of truth
- Integrate HubSpot with third-party tools via native integrations, APIs, and middleware (e.g., Make.com, Zapier, or custom scripts)

**Automation and Process Engineering**

- Automate the renewal lifecycle: build 90/60/30-day reminder sequences, renewal risk scoring, and AI-generated value realization summaries per account
- Design and implement lifecycle stage management, lead scoring, and marketing-to-sales handoff automation
- Create attribution models that connect marketing spend to pipeline and revenue outcomes
- Document every process you build. If it’s not documented, it doesn’t exist

**AI-Assisted Operations**

- Identify opportunities to apply AI tooling (LLMs, agentic workflows, AI-assisted analysis) to GTM operations
- Build or prototype internal tools that reduce manual work: automated deal intelligence, AI-powered customer health scoring, dynamic pricing analysis
- Serve as the bridge between our engineering team’s AI capabilities and the operational needs of Sales, Marketing, and Finance

**About You**

You are a builder who thinks in systems, not tickets. You care about data integrity as much as revenue impact. You would rather automate a process once than execute it manually a hundred times.

**Technical Requirements**

- 3+ years of hands-on experience building and architecting complex HubSpot environments (custom objects, advanced workflow automation, API integrations, and opportunity configuration)
- Demonstrated ability to write HubSpot workflows, integrate external systems via APIs, and build operational tooling—not just configure settings in a GUI
- Proven track record designing lifecycle stages, attribution models, deal stage architectures, and revenue reporting frameworks
- Experience building executive-level dashboards including velocity metrics, win/loss analysis, cohort retention, and forecasting
- Familiarity with AI/LLM tools and a demonstrated willingness to experiment with AI-assisted workflows (you don’t need to be an ML engineer, but you should be actively curious and using these tools in your work)
- Experience with .NET/C# strongly preferred, sufficient to build integrations, data pipelines, or internal tools

**How You Work**

- Ownership and initiative. You identify leverage points and drive them to completion without waiting to be told what to fix
- Engineering mindset. You approach operational problems the way a software engineer approaches code: with version control thinking, testing, documentation, and a bias toward automation over manual processes
- Collaboration and humility. You work across functions, welcome feedback, and hold a confident point of view without ego. You’ll work closely with Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, Finance, and Engineering
- Bias for clarity. You document processes, define terms, and reduce ambiguity in systems and reporting. If stakeholders can’t trust the data, none of the automation matters
- Comfort with ambiguity. Duende is a ~25-person company scaling rapidly. You’ll be building infrastructure from the ground up in some areas. That should excite you, not intimidate you

**What Success Looks Like**

**In your first 90 days:**

- HubSpot is configured for the new pricing and packaging structure. Deal stages, properties, and pipeline views reflect how we actually sell. You’ve audited existing data quality and have a remediation plan.
- Reporting dashboards are live and trusted at the board level. Renewals automation initiatives are in process; manual renewal communications are eliminated. You’ve integrated at least one external tool into the HubSpot ecosystem.
- The operational foundation for our June product launch is in place. Pipeline tracking, forecasting, and KPI reporting run without manual intervention. You’ve shipped at least one AI-assisted operational tool or workflow.

**Team, Salary and Location**

While the listed location is Austin, Texas, we have a worldwide, distributed team and will consider remote and flexible work arrangements worldwide. Duende’s compensation package includes a market-competitive salary ($90,000 - $130,000) and benefits, the specifics of which will be commensurate with candidate experience and fit.

﻿
Please email resumes to
careers@duendesoftware.com

We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to providing a positive interview experience for every candidate. If accommodations due to a disability or medical condition are needed, please let us know.

data.description:
**About Altruist**

Altruist is transforming the multi-trillion dollar wealth management industry by building an AI platform for wealth professionals. We partner with financial advisors nationwide, empowering them to grow, optimize time and resources, and deliver superior outcomes for their clients.

We're looking for exceptional talent to help us achieve our mission of making financial advice better, more affordable, and accessible to all. If you're passionate about challenging the status quo and want to do the most important work of your life, we'd love to meet you!

**But first, our values**

**Kindness** - Kindness doesn't *just* equal niceness. We listen to understand. We embrace, and encourage healthy debate and diverse perspectives. We approach conflict openly, honestly, and respectfully.  
  
**Brilliance** - Humility is the skill we're most proud of and possessing a growth mindset is always top of mind. We take ownership in everything we touch; regularly using our unique superpowers to reach a common goal as a team. We succeed and fail as one.  
  
**Grit** - When challenges arise, we stay laser focused on achieving our mission and finding a way forward, even when it's hard. We are nimble and maintain a sense of urgency, swiftly adapting to change and overcoming obstacles.

**About Hazel**

Hazel is building the AI engine for wealth management that helps unlock 10x growth, efficiency and value for financial advisors and their clients in a regulated industry. Since its launch last September, Hazel has organically and rapidly grown its user base.

Hazel is a part of Altruist's broader mission to make financial advice better, more affordable, and accessible to all.

**The opportunity**

Architect the commercial infrastructure for Hazel. You'll sit at the intersection of sales, marketing, product, and data, turning GTM strategy into automated systems that generate pipeline, accelerate deals, and surface the insights that drive our business strategy. As one of the first GTM Engineers at Hazel, you'll build foundational systems that will power our growth for years. If you love writing code as much as you love closing revenue loops — and you want to do it inside one of the most exciting AI companies in fintech — this is the role for you.

**What you'll do**

- Design, build, and own Hazel's GTM tech stack – including CRM (HubSpot/Salesforce), marketing automation, data enrichment, and outbound sequencing tools.
- Build automated workflows and AI-powered systems to support lead scoring, lifecycle marketing, outbound prospecting, and account-based motion.
- Write Python and/or JavaScript to connect data sources, automate enrichment pipelines, and extend CRM capabilities through APIs and custom integrations.
- Instrument the full funnel – from top-of-funnel awareness through activation and expansion – to surface actionable insights and revenue attribution to GTM leadership.
- Partner with Sales, Marketing, and Product to translate business needs into scalable technical solutions; own delivery end-to-end.
- Build and maintain a clean, reliable data foundation in our revenue data warehouse, enabling accurate forecasting and pipeline analytics.
- Evaluate and implement AI tools (e.g., Clay, Apollo, Instantly, and emerging LLM-based GTM tools) to amplify rep productivity and pipeline quality.
- Reduce manual ops overhead by automating repetitive GTM tasks so the team can focus on high-leverage activities.
- Willingness and comfort serving as technical support for AEs on sales calls.

**What you'll bring**

- 2-4 years of experience in Product/GTM Engineering at a high-growth SaaS or fintech company.
- Demonstrated ability to write production-quality code in Python, TypeScript or Javascript to build integrations, automation workflows, and data pipelines.
- Have built or prototyped AI-powered workflows using LLM APIs, Claude Code and/or OpenClaw.
- Hands-on experience with CRM platforms (HubSpot or Salesforce), marketing automation, and sales engagement tools (Outreach, Salesloft, Apollo, or similar).
- Strong SQL skills and comfort working with data warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, or Databricks) to build revenue dashboards and funnel analytics.
- Experience with data enrichment and orchestration tools (Clay, Clearbit, ZoomInfo, or similar) to build intelligent lead flows.
- A systems-thinker who can design scalable GTM architecture, not just patch point solutions.
- Strong cross-functional communication skills – you'll translate between business stakeholders and technical systems regularly.
- Comfort with ambiguity and early-stage pace – you move fast, prioritize ruthlessly, and iterate on the fly.

**Bonus points**

- Experience building GTM systems for AI-native products, where the sales motion and buyer education are non-standard.
- Experience with LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, or similar) to build AI-powered GTM workflows – lead qualification agents, email personalization, call summarization, etc.
- Background in data engineering or analytics engineering (dbt, Fivetran, Segment, or similar).
- Prior experience at an early-stage startup where you were the first or one of the first technical hires on the GTM side.

**Leveling & compensation**

This role has a target compensation range of $200,000-$225,000. Final level and compensation is individualized and will be determined based on overall experience, qualifications, and interview performance.

**What we bring**

Attracting and retaining top-tier talent is a priority. We are proud of the culture we've built and are cognizant of the ever-changing professional landscape. Our dynamic offering of perks and benefits are tailored for you to feel your best while doing your best.

- A hybrid work schedule for most positions to promote strong, in-person collaboration.
- Stunning, amenity-filled office spaces in Culver City, CA, San Francisco, CA, and Dallas, TX. Our offices are intentionally designed for comfort, collaboration, and productivity.
- Competitive pay and equity for eligible positions.
- Premium healthcare, dental, and vision insurance plans (HMO and PPO).
- 401k savings plan with a 4% match and immediate vesting.
- 16 week paid parental leave.
- Professional growth and development opportunities including an employee mobility program and an annual L&D budget allocation for each employee.
- Company perks program (includes discounts on pet insurance, fitness, cell phone plans, and travel, etc.).
- Financial guidance program (includes counseling on navigating debt, tracking personal spend, saving and planning goals, home-purchasing preparedness, etc.).
- One month work from anywhere policy (with the exception of a few countries).

*Total compensation includes a competitive benefits package, along with equity in the form of Stock Options (ISOs) for eligible roles. For salaried positions, a salary offer will be determined by a number of factors including experience, skill level, internal pay equity, geographic location, and other relevant business considerations. We review all employee pay and compensation programs regularly to ensure* *fair, equitable, and competitive pay. At Altruist, we are committed to providing fair, equitable, and competitive compensation by leveraging market data to inform our pay bands. Base salaries will be reviewed at regular intervals throughout the year, typically in conjunction with performance review cycles. By evaluating compensation on a regular basis, we are able to reward high performance and ensure all employees have opportunities for growth.*

*Don't meet every single requirement? Studies have shown that women and people of color are less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single qualification. At Altruist we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace, so if you're excited about this role, but your past experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyways. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.*

**About Us**

Weekend (formerly Volley) is the leading developer of voice AI games for smart TVs. Our games attract millions of users every month, with family favorites like Jeopardy!, Song Quiz, CoComelon: Sing and Play with JJ, and Wheel of Fortune.

We believe voice interfaces will become the main way people access entertainment in their living rooms, kitchens, bedrooms, and cars. We’re building a bundled subscription product similar to Netflix or Spotify for the emerging category of “AI-powered games.”

Weekend was founded by Max Child and James Wilsterman in 2024. The founders went Y Combinator in 2018 and landed a spot on the 2022 YC Top Companies List. We’re a quickly-growing team headquartered in Union Square, San Francisco.

**Role Summary**

Weekend is seeking a Senior Software Engineer to join our Hub Team, where you'll drive revenue growth through experimentation and data-driven optimization and own the funnel for acquisition, adoption and retention. This is a high-impact role for someone who thrives at the intersection of engineering, product, and analytics - and wants to see their work directly translate to business outcomes.

**What You’ll Do at Weekend**

- Own the complete experimentation lifecycle from hypothesis to implementation to analysis.
- Make changes to our client-side applications (Alexa, Roku, iOS, Android) to enable and optimize experiments.
- Partner with Growth, Data, and Product teams to identify and prioritize high-impact experiments.
- Lead cross-team projects including tracking infrastructure, MarTech stack enhancements, and Hub/game features.
- Evaluate and implement tools and system changes needed to support growth capabilities.
- Improve ad attribution systems to help teams understand campaign performance.
- Become the growth engineering expert and raise the capabilities of other engineers.

**We're Excited About You Because You Have**

- 5-8 years of software engineering experience with growth engineering focus.
- Proven track record running A/B tests and shipping features based on experimental data.
- Full-stack development expertise (TypeScript, React, Node.js/Bun preferred).
- Strong experience with analytics and experimentation platforms (Amplitude, Segment, Snowflake, Tableau).
- Familiarity with attribution and marketing tools (AppsFlyer, Branch, or similar).
- Deep understanding of conversion funnels, user journeys, and subscription business models.
- Strong project management skills and ability to drive initiatives across teams.
- Data-driven decision-making with user-first mentality.
- Comfort operating in ambiguous environments.

**Bonus Points**

- Experience in gaming or entertainment industries.
- Background implementing pixels and tracking for Meta, TikTok, and other ad. platforms.
- Experience with Klaviyo, Signal, or other MarTech tools.
- Understanding of subscription retention and monetization strategies.

**Our Stack**

- Typescript & Node.js
- React
- Kubernetes, Github Actions
- DynamoDB, PostgreSQL
- Segment, Amplitude
- Snowflake, Tableau

**The Hiring Process**

- **Stage 1 (Preliminary Video Call):**
  with a member of our recruitment team
- **Stage 2 (Engineering Panel):**
  with various members of our engineering team
- **Stage 3 (Onsite Interview):**
  with various partners and team members
- **Stage 4**
  **(Founder Chat)**
  with our Founders

As an AI company, we’re a huge believer in the value of AI tools. That being said, the interview process is intended as a conversation - we want to get to know you and your experience. Please refrain from using AI tools during all Weekend interviews unless otherwise specified

**What’s in it for You**

- Your medical, dental, and vision insurance is 100% covered by Weekend, for yourself and all dependents and we match 401k!
- We offer a flexible, hybrid work-from-home and in-office work model.
- We offer 4 weeks of paid time off (enforced 2-week minimum), 16 paid holidays, 40 hours of bereavement leave, and unlimited sick days.
- Our generous parental leave policy includes 24 weeks at 100% pay for birthing parents and 12 weeks at 100% pay for non-birthing parents.
- We offer visa support for eligible candidates, making relocating for the job smoother and more streamlined.

**About Syncari**

Syncari is modernizing the enterprise with autonomous master data management (MDM). We’re disrupting the multibillion‑dollar data‑management industry by redefining how enterprises build a trusted, AI‑ready data foundation. As the only agentic MDM platform, Syncari is leading a new path, eliminating silos, enforcing integrity, and enabling automation with continuous trust. 

Trusted by companies like Altair, Trimble, and Monotype, we help teams transform fragmented data into intelligent, governed systems that scale. Learn more at syncari.com.

**The Opportunity**

We are seeking a
**GTM Systems Engineer**

to help us deliver on our promise: to eliminate the chaos of fragmented data and enable confident, real-time decision-making.

*The GTM Systems Engineer will own data and systems lifecycle for Syncari's internal revenue operations, from integration and governance to activation. You'll build and maintain the technical infrastructure that connects our GTM stack, ensure data quality and consistency, and create the unified data foundation that powers analytics and decision-making across sales, marketing, and customer success.*

You'll report to the Chief of Staff & Operations and collaborate across Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success.

**What You'll Do**

- Own unified data management across our GTM stack, designing data models, enforcing quality rules, and implementing governance frameworks that ensure data accuracy and consistency.
- Build and maintain integrations using APIs and webhooks via Syncari to connect HubSpot, Gong, Warmly, Zendesk, Jira, and Amplemarket.
- Design ETL/ELT pipelines and data transformations to centralize, enrich, and activate data in real-time.
- Create automated workflows for lead routing, territory assignment, opportunity management, and customer lifecycle processes.
- Develop dashboards and reports that surface critical KPIs for revenue operations, pipeline health, and data quality metrics.
- Monitor and optimize system performance, data integrity, and integration reliability through audits, testing, and continuous improvement.
- Collaborate cross-functionally to translate business requirements into technical solutions and ensure systems support GTM goals.

**What You Bring**

- 4+ years in business systems, GTM automations or systems integration at a B2B SaaS company
- Hands-on experience administering HubSpot, Salesforce, and marketing automation tools
- Strong ETL/ELT skills for CRM and marketing data pipelines; focus on data quality and integrity
- Solid grasp of data modeling, MDM, and data governance in revenue systems
- Proficient in APIs, webhooks, REST, OAuth, and middleware tools (e.g., Syncari, Workato, Tray.io, Zapier)
- Skilled in workflow automation and scalable process design
- SQL and scripting (Python/JavaScript) for data transformation and integrations
- Experience with BI tools (Looker, Tableau, Power BI) to support GTM analytics
- Clear communicator with ability to align technical solutions to business needs

**What We Offer**

- Best-in-class health insurance (seriously—we invest heavily here)
- Flexible PTO and remote-first flexibility (U.S.-based)
- Competitive compensation + generous equity packages
- Real ownership: You'll be part of a passionate, mission-driven team shaping the future of master data

**Salary Range:**

The expected base salary for this role is $120,000 – $160,000 annually. Actual compensation may vary based on factors such as experience, qualifications, and geographic location.

data.description:
GTM Engineer @ Delve
  
  
Full-time
  
  

- In-Person
- San Francisco

**TL;DR:**
We need a technical, scrappy, systems-obsessed operator to join what we genuinely believe is one of the best growth teams on the planet. You'll work directly with our growth leadership to build the automations, data pipelines, and AI-first workflows that have helped us generate over
**$100M in pipeline in the last 12 months**
— at a compliance startup. Yeah, you read that right.
  
  
**About Delve**
Delve is building an AI-native platform that transforms compliance from tedious, manual work into effortless, automated workflows. We're the fastest growing compliance company on the market — helping hundreds of companies save hundreds of hours (Lovable, 11x, WisprFlow, and many more) by eliminating compliance busywork so they can focus on what actually matters: securely supporting their customers.
  
  
Our team is a tight-knit group of builders — former founders, operators, and engineers — obsessed with solving real problems for real customers. We come from Stanford, MIT, Berkeley, OpenAI, and international olympiads. Backed by
**Insight Partners**
,
**General Catalyst**
,
**Y Combinator**
, and top-tier investors, we just raised our
**$32M Series A**
and are scaling quickly.
  
  
One step at a time, we're on a mission to eliminate busywork for humanity.
  
  
Why this role matters
  
  
Our growth team is world-famous. Not "we put it on a slide deck" famous —
**actually famous**
. We're known for our growth hacks, our brand, our culture, and the absolutely ridiculous speed at which we execute. We've outbounded state senators on vacation. We've closed six-figure deals from a surf house in Hawaii. We've turned compliance —
*compliance*
— into something people actually talk about on Twitter.
  
  
But here's the thing: we're just getting started, and our systems need to scale as fast as our ambition. That's where you come in.
  
  
Your north star is simple:
**reactivate deals and generate demos.**
Everything you build, every workflow you ship, every automation you dream up — it all ladders up to putting more qualified conversations on our AEs' calendars and making sure no deal ever dies quietly in the CRM.
  
  
What you'll do
  
  
**Build the pipeline machine.**
You'll design and ship nurture sequences that keep prospects warm and move them through the funnel — not generic drip campaigns, but smart, signal-driven sequences that hit the right person with the right message at the right time. You'll also build reactivation sequences that breathe life back into closed-lost and stalled deals, because there's a goldmine sitting in our CRM and we need someone to go extract it.
  
  
**Arm our AEs for battle.**
You'll build cold calling cockpits that give our ramping AEs everything they need to pick up the phone with confidence — account context, talking points, recent activity, competitive intel, all in one view. You'll also create automatic morning deal briefings so every AE walks into their demos knowing exactly what's happening with each account, what to watch for, and how to close.
  
  
**Be our eyes on the funnel.**
You'll set up real-time alerts and notifications for high-value signals — logos entering the router, growing customers hitting expansion triggers, champion job changes, intent spikes. If something important happens in our pipeline, you'll make sure the right person knows about it before they've finished their coffee.
  
  
**Own and orchestrate our GTM stack.**
We run a serious toolkit — Default, Unify, Clay, HeyGen, HeyReach, and more. You'll be the person who makes all of these tools actually talk to each other, building the integrations, data flows, and automations that turn a collection of point solutions into a single, high-performance GTM engine. You'll evaluate new tools, sunset what isn't working, and make sure everything runs clean.
  
  
**Systematize what's working.**
We move fast and break things (in a good way). You'll take the scrappy plays that are generating pipeline and turn them into repeatable, scalable systems. Scoring, routing, enrichment, lifecycle management, usage signals — you'll design and ship all of it.
  
  
**Run experiments, scale the winners.**
$100M in pipeline is great. We want more. You'll dream up and execute differentiated outbound campaigns, A/B test everything, track what hits, kill what doesn't, and scale the winners fast. Every experiment should have a clear line to demos booked or deals reactivated.
  
  
**Make us more AI-first than we already are.**
We sell AI. We should be using it everywhere. You'll constantly find new ways to replace manual work with agents, workflows, and automation across every GTM function — from personalized video outreach with HeyGen to AI-powered account research to automated QBR prep.
  
  
**Partner across the org.**
Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, Product — you'll work across all of them. You'll sit with AEs to understand what's slowing them down, shadow demos to understand what's converting, and talk to CS to figure out which accounts are ready to expand. No silos. No politics. Just speed.
  
  
Your core KPIs
  
  
Let's be real about what success looks like:
  
  

- Demos generated — net new qualified meetings on AE calendars from your outbound, nurture, and reactivation workflows
- Deals reactivated — closed-lost and stalled opps brought back to life and re-entering pipeline
- AE efficiency — time saved per rep per day through briefings, cockpits, and automation
- Pipeline velocity — how fast deals move through stages thanks to your systems and signals

If those numbers are going up and to the right, you're winning.
  
  
Who you are
  
  
You've got
**5+ years**
in GTM operations, RevOps, growth engineering, or something adjacent — and you've got the technical chops to back it up. You've actually
*built*
things: scoring models, routing logic, outbound infrastructure, enrichment pipelines, nurture engines, calling workflows. Not managed them. Built them.
  
  
You're deep in the modern GTM tooling world. Clay, Unify, Default, HeyGen, HeyReach, Zapier, n8n — you know what's best-in-class and you have strong opinions about it. You write SQL. You mess with APIs. You might even write scripts when the no-code tool isn't cutting it.
  
  
You're a systems thinker who simplifies complexity instead of adding to it. You have a bias for automation and an allergic reaction to manual processes. You thrive in fast-paced environments where priorities shift and you're expected to figure things out on your own.
  
  
You care about craft. Not just shipping — shipping
*well*
. The small details matter to you because you know that's where the compounding gains live.
  
  
**Bonus points if:**
you have a CS degree, you've worked at a high-growth B2B SaaS startup, you've built cold calling cockpits or deal intelligence systems before, or you've ever resurrected a dead pipeline and turned it into real revenue.
  
  
Why in-person in SF matters
  
  
This is a fully in-person role at our San Francisco office. Here's why we don't apologize for that:
  
  

- You'll work shoulder-to-shoulder with an exceptional team. Decisions happen live, not in backlogged async threads.
- A players work with A players. There's a buzzing energy in the office. Hit the whiteboard with anyone here and you'll understand why.
- Speed requires proximity. The kind of experiments we run and the pace at which we ship — that happens when you can tap someone on the shoulder, not schedule a Zoom.

**Compensation & Benefits**
**$140,000 – $220,000**
base salary (depending on experience) + meaningful equity in a rocketship.
  
  

- 100% medical, dental & vision coverage (for you; partial for dependents)
- 401k with employer match
- Unlimited PTO + federal holidays
- GrubHub stipend + all meals covered in-office
- Gym membership covered
- Frequent team dinners, events, and off-sites (yes, including the Hawaii hackathons)
- The chance to be an early member of a growth team that people will study for years
- A culture that values speed, creativity, and ownership over titles and bureaucracy

One more thing
  
  
We don't care where you went to school. We don't care about your resume formatting. We care about what you've built, how you think, and whether you can keep up. If you've ever looked at a stale CRM full of dead deals and thought "I could turn half of these into demos by next Friday" — we want to talk to you.
  
  
**Delve is an equal opportunity employer.**
We don't discriminate based on gender, ethnicity, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, race, or anything else. We want the best people, period.
